name: Chuck Mangione
abstract: Charles Frank Mangione ( man-JOH-nee; November 29, 1940 – July 22, 2025) was an American flugelhorn player, trumpeter, actor, and composer. He came to prominence as a member of Art Blakey's band in the 1960s, and later co-led the Jazz Brothers with his brother, Gap, achieving international success in 1978 with his jazz-pop single "Feels …

concerts.label: A Chuck Mangione Concert with Concert Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript contains no mention of Chuck Mangione; the detailed personal relationship described is with Kai Winding.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chazz Frichtel
about_quotes.evidence: Well I started working with Chuck in 2000... next thing I knew I was in New York and we were at SIR playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chazz Frichtel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 13, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: Lonnie Liston Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers for a while, and Chuck Mangione was playing trumpet in the group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lonnie Liston Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Joe LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: Woody’s band, Chuck’s band — Chuck Mangione’s band... all the different leaders that I worked for in New York
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe LaBarbera interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 15, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Ricker
about_quotes.evidence: I was lucky to play in his expanded group that did tours. We did tours of the East Coast and Midwest.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Ricker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 27, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Ted Piltzecker
about_quotes.evidence: And that’s the band I played with. I was playing fifth trumpet. And Chuck Mangione was the director.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ted Piltzecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 10, 2019
